summary: DeVault delivers a comprehensive takedown of cryptocurrency, prompted by sourcehut's decision to require paid accounts for CI services due to rampant crypto mining abuse. He argues that cryptocurrency has invented an entirely new category of internet abuse, forcing free service providers to shut down public resources. Beyond the direct abuse, he contends that crypto has corrupted the software industry's incentive structures, turning promising projects into thinly veiled get-rich-quick schemes. He dismisses claims that cryptocurrency serves as a legitimate currency, calling it an investment vehicle that enriches the wealthy while exploiting vulnerable communities and wasting enormous amounts of energy. He concludes that cryptocurrency is one of the worst inventions of the 21st century.
key_points: Cryptocurrency mining abuse has forced sourcehut and other CI providers to eliminate free CPU resources, destroying legitimate public services
The software industry's trust and integrity have declined as crypto creates perverse incentives where developers push projects to get rich rather than to solve problems
Cryptocurrency has failed as an actual currency and functions only as an investment vehicle with more in common with a Ponzi scheme than a genuine investment
Communities near power plants suffer blackouts while miners consume electricity and dump CO₂ into the atmosphere, exploiting rather than helping failed economies
Any attempts at reform like proof-of-stake are blocked by those in power who refuse to risk their bottom line
money_quotes: Cryptocurrency has invented an entirely new category of internet abuse.
Someone found a way of monetizing stolen CPU cycles directly, so everyone who offered free CPU cycles for legitimate use-cases is now unable to provide those services.
Cryptocurrency is the multi-level marketing of the tech world.
Any technology which is not an (alleged) currency and which incorporates blockchain anyway would always work better without it.
That's what cryptocurrency is all about: not novel technology, not empowerment, but making money.
No, cryptocurrency does not help failed states. It exploits them.
When you're the only honest person in the room, maybe you should be in a different room.
Cryptocurrency is one of the worst inventions of the 21st century. I am ashamed to share an industry with this exploitative grift.
key_insight: Cryptocurrency has monetized stolen computing resources, corrupted software industry incentives, and functions as an exploitative Ponzi scheme rather than the empowering technology its proponents claim.
